verses.latin_vulgate: Quis credidit auditui nostro ?  et brachium Domini cui revelatum est ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִ֥י הֶאֱמִ֖ין לִשְׁמֻעָתֵ֑נוּ וּזְר֥וֹעַ יְהֹוָ֖ה עַל־מִ֥י נִגְלָֽתָה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Who a hath believed our report? and to whom is the arm of the Lord revealed?
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuka uskoo meidän saarnamme, ja kenelle Herran käsivarsi ilmoitetaan?
verses.king_james: Who hath believed our report? and to whom is the arm of the Lord revealed?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ascendet sicut virgultum coram eo,  et sicut radix de terra sitienti.  Non est species ei, neque decor,  et vidimus eum,  et non erat aspectus, et desideravimus eum :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֨עַל כַּיּוֹנֵ֜ק לְפָנָ֗יו וְכַשֹּׁ֙רֶשׁ֙ מֵאֶ֣רֶץ צִיָּ֔ה לֹא־תֹ֥אַר ל֖וֹ וְלֹ֣א הָדָ֑ר וְנִרְאֵ֥הוּ וְלֹֽא־מַרְאֶ֖ה וְנֶחְמְדֵֽהוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he shall grow up as a tender plant before him, and as a root out of a thirsty ground: there is no beauty in him, nor comeliness: and we have seen him, and there was no sightliness, that we should be desirous of him:
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä hän nousee ylös hänen edessänsä niinkuin vesa, ja niinkuin juuri kuivasta maasta; ei hänellä ole muotoa, eikä kauneutta; me näimme hänen, vaan ei ollut hänellä sitä muotoa, joka meille olis kelvannut.
verses.king_james: For he shall grow up before him as a tender plant, and as a root out of a dry ground: he hath no form nor comeliness; and when we shall see him, there is no beauty that we should desire him.

verses.latin_vulgate: despectum, et novissimum virorum,  virum dolorum, et scientem infirmitatem,  et quasi absconditus vultus ejus et despectus,  unde nec reputavimus eum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נִבְזֶה֙ וַחֲדַ֣ל אִישִׁ֔ים אִ֥ישׁ מַכְאֹב֖וֹת וִיד֣וּעַ חֹ֑לִי וּכְמַסְתֵּ֤ר פָּנִים֙ מִמֶּ֔נּוּ נִבְזֶ֖ה וְלֹ֥א חֲשַׁבְנֻֽהוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Despised, and the most abject of men, a man of sorrows, and acquainted with infirmity: and his look was as it were hidden and despised, whereupon we esteemed him not.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän oli kaikkein ylönkatsottava ja kaikkein halvin, kipua ja sairautta täynnä. Hän oli niin ylönkatsottu, ettei kenkään kehdannut katsoa hänen päällensä, sentähden emme häntä minäkään pitäneet.
verses.king_james: He is despised and rejected of men; a man of sorrows, and acquainted with grief: and we hid as it were our faces from him; he was despised, and we esteemed him not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vere languores nostros ipse tulit,  et dolores nostros ipse portavit ;  et nos putavimus eum quasi leprosum,  et percussum a Deo, et humiliatum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אָכֵ֤ן חֳלָיֵ֙נוּ֙ ה֣וּא נָשָׂ֔א וּמַכְאֹבֵ֖ינוּ סְבָלָ֑ם וַאֲנַ֣חְנוּ חֲשַׁבְנֻ֔הוּ נָג֛וּעַ מֻכֵּ֥ה אֱלֹהִ֖ים וּמְעֻנֶּֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Surely he hath borne our infirmities and carried our sorrows: and we have thought him as it were a leper, and as one struck by God and afflicted.
verses.finnish_biblia: Totisesti hän kantoi meidän sairautemme, ja meidän kipumme hän sälytti päällensä; mutta me pidimme hänen Jumalalta rangaistuna, piestynä ja vaivattuna.
verses.king_james: Surely he hath borne our griefs, and carried our sorrows: yet we did esteem him stricken, smitten of God, and afflicted.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ipse autem vulneratus est propter iniquitates nostras ;  attritus est propter scelera nostra :  disciplina pacis nostræ super eum,  et livore ejus sanati sumus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהוּא֙ מְחֹלָ֣ל מִפְּשָׁעֵ֔נוּ מְדֻכָּ֖א מֵעֲוֺנֹתֵ֑ינוּ מוּסַ֤ר שְׁלוֹמֵ֙נוּ֙ עָלָ֔יו וּבַחֲבֻרָת֖וֹ נִרְפָּא־לָֽנוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: But he was wounded for our iniquities, he was bruised for our sins: the chastisement of our peace was upon him, and by his bruises we are healed.
verses.finnish_biblia: Vaan hän on haavoitettu meidän pahain tekoimme tähden, ja meidän synteimme tähden on hän hosuttu. Rangaistus on hänen päällänsä, että meillä rauha olis, ja hänen haavainsa kautta olemme me parannetut.
verses.king_james: But he was wounded for our transgressions, he was bruised for our iniquities: the chastisement of our peace was upon him; and with his stripes we are healed.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnes nos quasi oves erravimus,  unusquisque in viam suam declinavit :  et posuit Dominus in eo  iniquitatem omnium nostrum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֻּלָּ֙נוּ֙ כַּצֹּ֣אן תָּעִ֔ינוּ אִ֥ישׁ לְדַרְכּ֖וֹ פָּנִ֑ינוּ וַיהֹוָה֙ הִפְגִּ֣יעַ בּ֔וֹ אֵ֖ת עֲוֺ֥ן כֻּלָּֽנוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: All we like sheep have gone astray, every one hath turned aside into his own way: and the Lord hath laid on him the iniquity of us all.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kaikki me vaelsimme eksyksissä niinkuin lampaat: itsekukin meistä poikkesi omalle tiellensä; mutta Herra heitti kaiken meidän vääryytemme hänen päällensä.
verses.king_james: All we like sheep have gone astray; we have turned every one to his own way; and the Lord hath laid on him the iniquity of us all.

verses.latin_vulgate: Oblatus est quia ipse voluit,  et non aperuit os suum ;  sicut ovis ad occisionem ducetur,  et quasi agnus coram tondente se obmutescet,  et non aperiet os suum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נִגַּ֨שׂ וְה֣וּא נַעֲנֶה֮ וְלֹ֣א יִפְתַּח־פִּיו֒ כַּשֶּׂה֙ לַטֶּ֣בַח יוּבָ֔ל וּכְרָחֵ֕ל לִפְנֵ֥י גֹזְזֶ֖יהָ נֶאֱלָ֑מָה וְלֹ֥א יִפְתַּ֖ח פִּֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: He was offered because it was his own will, and he opened not his mouth: he shall be led as a sheep to the slaughter, and shall be dumb as a lamb before his shearer, and he shall not open his mouth.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuin hän rangaistiin ja vaivattiin, niin ei hän suutansa avannut; niinkuin karitsa, joka teuraaksi viedään, ja niinkuin lammas, joka keritsiänsä edessä vaikenee, niin ei hänkään suutansa avannut.
verses.king_james: He was oppressed, and he was afflicted, yet he opened not his mouth: he is brought as a lamb to the slaughter, and as a sheep before her shearers is dumb, so he openeth not his mouth.

verses.latin_vulgate: De angustia, et de judicio sublatus est.  Generationem ejus quis enarrabit ?  quia abscissus est de terra viventium :  propter scelus populi mei percussi eum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מֵעֹ֤צֶר וּמִמִּשְׁפָּט֙ לֻקָּ֔ח וְאֶת־דּוֹר֖וֹ מִ֣י יְשׂוֹחֵ֑חַ כִּ֤י נִגְזַר֙ מֵאֶ֣רֶץ חַיִּ֔ים מִפֶּ֥שַׁע עַמִּ֖י נֶ֥גַע לָֽמוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: He was taken away from distress, and from judgment: who shall declare his generation? because he is cut off out of the land of the living: for the wickedness of my people have I struck him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta hän on otettu pois ahdistuksesta ja tuomiosta; kuka taitaa sanoa hänen elämänsä pituuden? sillä hän on sivallettu pois eläväin maalta, kuin hän minun kansani pahain tekoin tähden rangaistiin.
verses.king_james: He was taken from prison and from judgment: and who shall declare his generation? for he was cut off out of the land of the living: for the transgression of my people was he stricken.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dabit impios pro sepultura,  et divitem pro morte sua,  eo quod iniquitatem non fecerit,  neque dolus fuerit in ore ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּתֵּ֤ן אֶת־רְשָׁעִים֙ קִבְר֔וֹ וְאֶת־עָשִׁ֖יר בְּמֹתָ֑יו עַ֚ל לֹא־חָמָ֣ס עָשָׂ֔ה וְלֹ֥א מִרְמָ֖ה בְּפִֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he shall give the ungodly for his burial, and the rich for his death: because he hath done no iniquity, neither was there deceit in his mouth.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hänen hautansa aiottiin jumalattomain sekaan, rikkaan tykö tuli hän kuitenkin kuoltuansa. Ei hän kellenkään vääryyttä tehnyt, eikä petosta ollut hänen suussansa.
verses.king_james: And he made his grave with the wicked, and with the rich in his death; because he had done no violence, neither was any deceit in his mouth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et Dominus voluit conterere eum in infirmitate.  Si posuerit pro peccato animam suam,  videbit semen longævum,  et voluntas Domini in manu ejus dirigetur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיהֹוָ֞ה חָפֵ֤ץ דַּכְּאוֹ֙ הֶחֱלִ֔י אִם־תָּשִׂ֤ים אָשָׁם֙ נַפְשׁ֔וֹ יִרְאֶ֥ה זֶ֖רַע יַאֲרִ֣יךְ יָמִ֑ים וְחֵ֥פֶץ יְהֹוָ֖ה בְּיָד֥וֹ יִצְלָֽח׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the Lord was pleased to bruise him in infirmity: if he shall lay down his life for sin, he shall see a longlived seed, and the will of the Lord shall be prosperous in his hand.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Herra tahtoi hänen niin piestä sairaudella; koska hän on henkensä vikauhriksi antanut, niin hän saa nähdä siemenen ja elää kauvan, ja Herran aivoitus on hänen kädessänsä menestyvä.
verses.king_james: Yet it pleased the Lord to bruise him; he hath put him to grief: when thou shalt make his soul an offering for sin, he shall see his seed, he shall prolong his days, and the pleasure of the Lord shall prosper in his hand.

verses.latin_vulgate: Pro eo quod laboravit anima ejus,  videbit et saturabitur.  In scientia sua justificabit  ipse justus servus meus multos,  et iniquitates eorum ipse portabit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מֵעֲמַ֤ל נַפְשׁוֹ֙ יִרְאֶ֣ה יִשְׂבָּ֔ע בְּדַעְתּ֗וֹ יַצְדִּ֥יק צַדִּ֛יק עַבְדִּ֖י לָרַבִּ֑ים וַעֲוֺנֹתָ֖ם ה֥וּא יִסְבֹּֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: Because his soul hath laboured, he shall see and be filled: by his knowledge shall this my just servant justify many, and he shall bear their iniquities.
verses.finnish_biblia: Että hänen sielunsa on työtä tehnyt, saa hän ilonsa nähdä, ja tulee ravituksi. Tuntemisensa kautta minun vanhurskas palveliani monta vanhurskauttaa; sillä hän kantaa heidän syntinsä.
verses.king_james: He shall see of the travail of his soul, and shall be satisfied: by his knowledge shall my righteous servant justify many; for he shall bear their iniquities.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ideo dispertiam ei plurimos,  et fortium dividet spolia,  pro eo quod tradidit in mortem animam suam,  et cum sceleratis reputatus est,  et ipse peccata multorum tulit,  et pro transgressoribus rogavit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָכֵ֞ן אֲחַלֶּק־ל֣וֹ בָרַבִּ֗ים וְאֶת־עֲצוּמִים֮ יְחַלֵּ֣ק שָׁלָל֒ תַּ֗חַת אֲשֶׁ֨ר הֶעֱרָ֤ה לַמָּ֙וֶת֙ נַפְשׁ֔וֹ וְאֶת־פֹּשְׁעִ֖ים נִמְנָ֑ה וְהוּא֙ חֵטְא־רַבִּ֣ים נָשָׂ֔א וְלַפֹּשְׁעִ֖ים יַפְגִּֽיעַ׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: Therefore will I distribute to him very many, and he shall divide the spoils of the strong, because he hath delivered his soul unto death, and was reputed with the wicked: and he hath borne the sins of many, and hath prayed for the transgressors.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sentähden annan minä hänelle osan monessa, ja hänen pitää väkeväin kanssa saalista jakaman; sentähden että hän antoi henkensä kuolemaan, ja oli pahantekiäin sekaan luettu, ja hän kantoi monen synnit, ja rukoili pahantekiäin edestä.
verses.king_james: Therefore will I divide him a portion with the great, and he shall divide the spoil with the strong; because he hath poured out his soul unto death: and he was numbered with the transgressors; and he bare the sin of many, and made intercession for the transgressors.
